samples.gvd:
module serial2parallel (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input din_serial,
    input din_valid,
    output reg [7:0]dout_parallel,
    output reg dout_valid
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data (single bit).</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        The module converts serial input data into parallel output data. 
        On each rising edge of the clock, it counts up to 8. 
        Once 8 inputs are received, it sets dout_parallel to the collected data 
        and dout_valid to 1, indicating valid output. 
        If less than 8 inputs are received, dout_valid remains 0.
    </implementation>
</module>
